examples.sentence: 彼は犬が怖いかのようだ。
examples.translation: He seems as if he is scared of dogs.
examples.translationEn: He seems as if he is scared of dogs.
examples.romaji: Kare wa inu ga kowai ka no you da.

examples.sentence: この部屋は寒いかのようだ。
examples.translation: This room feels as if it's cold.
examples.translationEn: This room feels as if it's cold.
examples.romaji: Kono heya wa samui ka no you da.

examples.sentence: 彼女は有名人であるかのようだ。
examples.translation: She acts as though she is a celebrity.
examples.translationEn: She acts as though she is a celebrity.
examples.romaji: Kanojo wa yuumeijin de aru ka no you da.

examples.sentence: 彼は子供であるかのようだ。
examples.translation: He acts as if he's a child.
examples.translationEn: He acts as if he's a child.
examples.romaji: Kare wa kodomo de aru ka no you da.

examples.sentence: このプロジェクトを引き受けようか引き受けまいか、まだ決めていない。
examples.translation: I haven't decided whether to take on this project or not.
examples.translationEn: I haven't decided whether to take on this project or not.
examples.romaji: Kono purojekuto o hikiukeyou ka hikiukemai ka, mada kimete inai.

examples.sentence: 彼が成功しようか成功するまいか、今は予測できない。
examples.translation: Whether he will succeed or not is unpredictable at the moment.
examples.translationEn: Whether he will succeed or not is unpredictable at the moment.
examples.romaji: Kare ga seikou shiyou ka seikou suru maika, ima wa yosoku dekinai.

examples.sentence: この問題は簡単であるか難しいであるまいか、意見が分かれている。
examples.translation: Opinions are divided on whether this problem is simple or difficult.
examples.translationEn: Opinions are divided on whether this problem is simple or difficult.
examples.romaji: Kono mondai wa kantan de aru ka muzukashii de aru maika, iken ga wakarete iru.

examples.sentence: 彼はリーダーであるかフォロワーであるまいか、自分で選ぶべきだ。
examples.translation: He should choose for himself whether he is a leader or a follower.
examples.translationEn: He should choose for himself whether he is a leader or a follower.
examples.romaji: Kare wa riidā de aru ka forowā de aru maika, jibun de erabu beki da.

examples.sentence: 失敗したからこそ、成功の意味がわかる。
examples.translation: It is precisely because I failed that I understand the meaning of success.
examples.translationEn: It is precisely because I failed that I understand the meaning of success.
examples.romaji: Shippai shita kara koso, seikou no imi ga wakaru.

examples.sentence: 苦しかったからこそ、今日の私がある。
examples.translation: It is precisely because I went through hardships that I am who I am today.
examples.translationEn: It is precisely because I went through hardships that I am who I am today.
examples.romaji: Kurushikatta kara koso, kyou no watashi ga aru.

examples.sentence: 彼は親切だからこそ、人気がある。
examples.translation: He is popular precisely because he is kind.
examples.translationEn: He is popular precisely because he is kind.
examples.romaji: Kare wa shinsetsu da kara koso, ninki ga aru.

examples.sentence: 昔の経験があるからこそ、その問題がよく理解できる。
examples.translation: I can understand that problem well precisely because I have past experience.
examples.translationEn: I can understand that problem well precisely because I have past experience.
examples.romaji: Mukashi no keiken ga aru kara koso, sono mondai ga yoku rikai dekiru.

examples.sentence: 彼の顔つきからして、何か悪いことがあったのだろう。
examples.translation: From his facial expression, something bad must have happened.
examples.translationEn: From his facial expression, something bad must have happened.
examples.romaji: Kare no kaotsuki kara shite, nanika warui koto ga atta no darou.

examples.sentence: この料理の見た目からして、おいしくなさそうだ。
examples.translation: Judging from the appearance of this dish, it doesn't look delicious.
examples.translationEn: Judging from the appearance of this dish, it doesn't look delicious.
examples.romaji: Kono ryouri no mitame kara shite, oishikunasa sou da.

examples.sentence: 彼女の試験成績からして、合格するのは難しいでしょう。
examples.translation: Considering her test scores, it will be difficult for her to pass.
examples.translationEn: Considering her test scores, it will be difficult for her to pass.
examples.romaji: Kanojo no shiken seiseki kara shite, goukaku suru no wa muzukashii deshou.

examples.sentence: その映画のタイトルからして、面白くないと思う。
examples.translation: Judging from the title of the movie, I think it's not interesting.
examples.translationEn: Judging from the title of the movie, I think it's not interesting.
examples.romaji: Sono eiga no taitoru kara shite, omoshirokunai to omou.

examples.sentence: 経験からすると、この仕事は難しいでしょう。
examples.translation: Considering their experience, this job might be difficult.
examples.translationEn: Considering their experience, this job might be difficult.
examples.romaji: Keiken kara suru to, kono shigoto wa muzukashii deshou.

examples.sentence: 評判からすると、あのレストランはとても美味しいです。
examples.translation: Judging from the reputation, that restaurant is very delicious.
examples.translationEn: Judging from the reputation, that restaurant is very delicious.
examples.romaji: Hyouban kara suru to, ano resutoran wa totemo oishii desu.

examples.sentence: 価格からすると、この商品は高品質のものと思われます。
examples.translation: Considering the price, this product seems to be of high quality.
examples.translationEn: Considering the price, this product seems to be of high quality.
examples.romaji: Kakaku kara suru to, kono shouhin wa kouhinshitsu no mono to omowaremasu.

examples.sentence: このエリアですが、交通の便からすると、便利です。
examples.translation: As for this area, considering the transportation convenience, it's convenient.
examples.translationEn: As for this area, considering the transportation convenience, it's convenient.
examples.romaji: Kono eria desu ga, koutsuu no ben kara suru to, benri desu.

examples.sentence: 雨が降ったからといって、試合が中止になるわけではありません。
examples.translation: Just because it rained doesn't mean the game will be canceled.
examples.translationEn: Just because it rained doesn't mean the game will be canceled.
examples.romaji: Ame ga futta kara to itte, shiai ga chuushi ni naru wake de wa arimasen.

examples.sentence: 彼が有名だからといって、彼の意見がいつも正しいわけではない。
examples.translation: Just because he is famous doesn't mean his opinions are always correct.
examples.translationEn: Just because he is famous doesn't mean his opinions are always correct.
examples.romaji: Kare ga yuumei da kara to itte, kare no iken ga itsumo tadashii wake de wa nai.

examples.sentence: このスープが辛いからといって、全ての人が辛いと感じるわけではない。
examples.translation: Just because this soup is spicy doesn't mean everyone will find it spicy.
examples.translationEn: Just because this soup is spicy doesn't mean everyone will find it spicy.
examples.romaji: Kono suupu ga karai kara to itte, subete no hito ga karai to kanjiru wake de wa nai.

examples.sentence: 彼女が泣いているからといって、悲しいわけではないかもしれない。
examples.translation: Just because she is crying doesn't necessarily mean she's sad.
examples.translationEn: Just because she is crying doesn't necessarily mean she's sad.
examples.romaji: Kanojo ga naiteiru kara to itte, kanashii wake de wa nai kamoshirenai.

examples.sentence: 午前９時から午後５時にかけて、仕事をしています。
examples.translation: I work from 9 in the morning to 5 in the evening.
examples.translationEn: I work from 9 in the morning to 5 in the evening.
examples.romaji: Gozen ku-ji kara gogo go-ji ni kakete, shigoto wo shite imasu.

examples.sentence: 東京から大阪にかけて、新幹線が走っています。
examples.translation: The bullet train runs from Tokyo to Osaka.
examples.translationEn: The bullet train runs from Tokyo to Osaka.
examples.romaji: Toukyou kara Oosaka ni kakete, shinkansen ga hashitte imasu.

examples.sentence: 春から夏にかけて、花がたくさん咲いています。
examples.translation: Many flowers bloom between spring and summer.
examples.translationEn: Many flowers bloom between spring and summer.
examples.romaji: Haru kara natsu ni kakete, hana ga takusan saite imasu.

examples.sentence: ２０歳から３０歳にかけて、多くの人が大学を卒業し、仕事に就くことが一般的です。
examples.translation: It is common for many people to graduate from university and start working between the ages of 20 and 30.
examples.translationEn: It is common for many people to graduate from university and start working between the ages of 20 and 30.
examples.romaji: Hatachi kara sanjuu-sai ni kakete, ooku no hito ga daigaku wo sotsugyou shi, shigoto ni tsuku koto ga ippan-teki desu.

examples.sentence: 試験に合格したからには、一所懸命働かなければならない。
examples.translation: Since I passed the exam, I have to work hard.
examples.translationEn: Since I passed the exam, I have to work hard.
examples.romaji: Shiken ni goukaku shita kara niwa, isshokenmei hatarakanakereba naranai.

examples.sentence: 約束したからには、守らなければなりません。
examples.translation: Since I made a promise, I must keep it.
examples.translationEn: Since I made a promise, I must keep it.
examples.romaji: Yakusoku shita kara niwa, mamoranakereba narimasen.

examples.sentence: この映画が面白いからには、友達にも見せたい。
examples.translation: Since this movie is interesting, I want to show it to my friends too.
examples.translationEn: Since this movie is interesting, I want to show it to my friends too.
examples.romaji: Kono eiga ga omoshiroi kara niwa, tomodachi ni mo misetai.

examples.sentence: 彼女が助けを求めたからには、手伝わなければならない。
examples.translation: Since she asked for help, I must help her.
examples.translationEn: Since she asked for help, I must help her.
examples.romaji: Kanojo ga tasuke wo motometa kara niwa, tetsudawanakereba naranai.
